commit.message:
Prevent duplicate polling loops when reconnecting to running scan

Track polling interval in global variable currentPollInterval so we can
cancel any existing polling before starting a new one. This fixes the issue
where reconnecting multiple times would create multiple polling loops,
causing duplicate log messages.

Changes:
- Add currentPollInterval global variable
- Clear existing polling before starting new polling loop
- Set currentPollInterval to null when polling completes or times out
- Clear polling when user clicks reset button

This ensures only one polling loop runs at a time, preventing:
- Duplicate reconnect messages
- Duplicate progress updates
- Out-of-order elapsed time messages
- Resource waste from multiple simultaneous polling loops

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix FOV corner-clipping: block rays that squeeze through diagonal wall gaps

Bresenham's line makes diagonal steps (both x and y advance in one
iteration). When this happens, the ray was not checking the two
cardinal-neighbour tiles it passes between, allowing it to "see through"
touching room corners.

Fix: before each diagonal step, check both cardinal neighbours. If both
are walls (or out-of-bounds), return false — the ray is blocked.

Also extracted isWall() helper; out-of-bounds tiles are treated as opaque.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(keepalive): hold strong reference to ping task to prevent GC

asyncio.create_task returns a Task that the event loop holds only via a
weak reference. Without a strong reference the GC can silently discard
the task before it ever pings, making the keepalive a no-op.

Store the task in a module-level set and discard it via done_callback so
the reference is released cleanly when (if) the loop ever exits.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix build error: use CSS background-image instead of for hero sections Vite resolves paths as module imports at build time, causing Rollup to fail when the image files don't yet exist in public/images/. Using inline style background-image url() is not processed by Rollup, so the build succeeds and images load at runtime when added by the client. * feat(engine): v0.5 sprint — #29 #30 #31 #33 #34 + fast_mode dequantize fix

### #33 WAL write coalescing
Pre-build entire batch into one Vec<u8>, then one write_all + one flush
per append_batch call. Eliminates per-entry syscall overhead.

### #29 SIMD unpack_mse_indices
AVX2 variant for b=4: 16 packed bytes → 32 u16 per iteration via
_mm_unpacklo/hi_epi8 + _mm256_cvtepu8_epi16. Scalar fallback unchanged.

### #34 Cross-query parallelism in search_batch
For ANN or small-N (≤SEQ_THRESHOLD=20k) paths, par_iter() across queries.
Large-N brute-force stays serial (inner par_chunks saturates Rayon there).

### #31 Range index for metadata
BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u32>> per numeric field using IEEE 754 ordered-key
encoding (f64_to_ord). extract_range_condition() routes $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte
filters through get_range_candidates() in resolve_filter_via_index().

### #30 Auto query planner
_use_ann: Option<bool> (None=auto, True=force ANN, False=force brute).
auto_use_ann() returns true when index exists + N≥10k + delta≤20% of N.
Module-level constants: AUTO_ANN_THRESHOLD=10_000, AUTO_ANN_MAX_DELTA=20%.

### fix(quantizer): fast_mode dequantize
dequantize() short-circuits to MSE-only when fast_mode=True, preventing
a panic on the zero-length QJL slice during rerank.

* fix(python): change defaults to rerank=False, fast_mode=True (minimum disk out-of-box)

Previous defaults (rerank=True, fast_mode=False) stored INT8 raw vectors and QJL
residuals for every new database, making the zero-config experience heavier than
needed. New defaults match the paper Figure 5 allocation and minimize disk usage:

  rerank=False  — no raw vector storage; pure compressed codes
  fast_mode=True — MSE-only quantization; fastest ingest, minimum disk
  quantizer_type=None ("dense") — unchanged

Users who need higher recall explicitly opt in:
  rerank=True           → +5–25 pp R@1, ~2–4× more disk
  fast_mode=False       → +5–10 pp R@1 at d≥1536 when rerank=False

parameter table, fast_mode×rerank combination table, all preset examples,
and the decision flowchart.

* feat: persistent progress for saved setlists

Add active setlist tracking so checked-card progress persists
reliably across page reloads and config changes.

Key changes:
- Track activeSetlistId (persisted in localStorage) — loading a
  saved setlist activates it, giving it a stable storage key
  (setlist:${id}) instead of the volatile config-derived key
- Migrate legacy progress from old dynamic keys on first load
- Lift savedSetlists state into useCollectionState for centralized
  save/delete/update management
- Add Save/Save As/New buttons with active setlist visual indicator
- Add uniqueArtOnly to SavedSetlist type (was missing)
- Clean up progress key when deleting a setlist

- Quick wins: Ready to merge: next_action matches Ready to merge

The readmes publish process previously accepted a single 'root' or 'sdk'
mode that could overwrite local README files and lacked a safe restore path.

targeted approach was chosen over a single 'root' mode because it avoids
accidental loss of local changes.
